code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test17-OCT-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting possible renal dysfunction and/or hepatic involvement. Glucose and HbA1c are low, and there is mild ketonuria. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites, leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation for renal and hepatic pathology and assessment of glycemic status.
